description.p: HyprMod is a native GTK4/libadwaita settings app for Hyprland. Every change applies instantly to the running compositor, and your hand-crafted config stays untouched — HyprMod writes only to its own managed config file.

ul.li: Live preview — changes apply instantly, no reload needed
Monitor configuration with layout preview; VRR, HDR, and 10-bit detection
Bezier curve editor with draggable control points and live animation preview
Keybind editor with interactive key capture, including mouse-drag binds
Window rules and layer rules editors with live preview
Autostart and environment variable management
Pending Changes page — review every unsaved edit before saving
Cursor theme picker with live previews
Profiles — save, name, and switch between complete configurations
Config DNA — every profile gets a unique visual fingerprint
Global search across all options (Ctrl+F)
Undo with Ctrl+Z

screenshot.caption: Monitor configuration with multi-monitor layout preview
Bezier curve editor with live animation preview

description.p: Lua config support, new pages, and major fixes:

ul.li: Lua config support: read and edit hyprland.lua directly, with a "Migrate to Lua" wizard (Hyprland 0.55+)
Per-monitor "Identify by description" toggle for port-independent monitor configs
Advanced HDR controls: Color Management presets, SDR brightness/saturation, luminance sliders
Workspaces page: manage workspace rules with live preview and live IPC apply
Deprecation assistant: detect and migrate deprecated syntax with user confirmation and backups
Convenience installer (install.sh) and --install / --uninstall CLI flags
In-app bug reporting with prefilled GitHub issues
Per-monitor VRR control with "Use global" and corrected Off behavior
Named window and layer rules support

description.p: New configuration pages and UX improvements:

ul.li: Window Rules page — manage windowrule entries with a window picker and live preview
Layer Rules page — manage layerrule entries with curated presets and live preview
Autostart page — manage exec and exec-once entries with an app picker
Env Variables page — manage env entries with POSIX name validation
Pending Changes page — review every unsaved edit across the app in one place
Mouse-drag (bindm) keybinds with dispatcher, category, and preset support
Sidebar reorganized by task; Dwindle, Master, and Scrolling merged into a single Layouts page
Profiles page redesigned — active profile promoted to a hero card with the saved-profiles list below; cards show last-modified date
Saving keeps the active profile in sync automatically
